Tom, can you believe it? We are already halfway through our English course. It has been quite a journey.
I know! Do you remember your very first class? How did you feel back then?
So nervous! When I started, I could only say a few words. Now I have a much better routine, and I study almost every day.
That is a big change. What did you learn in those first lessons?
I learned to talk about my neighborhood, my favorite hobby, and my daily schedule. Simple things, but they gave me confidence.
And now? What has been the biggest milestone for you so far?
Honestly, the biggest milestone is how many mistakes I have overcome. Every class is an opportunity to grow.
So what is next? Do you have any big goals?
Yes! One day I am going to work abroad as a doctor. That dream is at the top of my bucket list.
When I started, I could only say a few words.
Learning English has been a long journey.
I have overcome many of my mistakes.
Now I can hold a real conversation.

I have been learning English for about a year now, and I still remember my very first class. When I started, I could barely introduce myself; I knew only a few words and I felt so nervous. My routine was already busy, so finding time to study was hard, but I decided to keep going. Little by little, I learned to talk about my neighborhood, my daily schedule, my hobbies, and my plans for the future. Looking back, the biggest highlight is not one single lesson; it is how much more confident I feel today. I have made a lot of mistakes, but I have overcome most of them, and each mistake taught me something. I am only halfway through my journey, and I already cannot wait for the second half.

Hi Ana Paula, it is Sarah! I just finished my midpoint review with my teacher, and it made me think about you. Can you believe how far we have both come? When we started, we could only say very simple sentences, and now we can actually have a real conversation. My teacher asked me about my goals for the second half of the course, and I realized I have so many. I want to travel abroad and use my English on the trip; that is right at the top of my bucket list. I still have things to improve, of course, but every class is a new opportunity. Anyway, tell me: what is the biggest milestone you have reached so far, and what do you want to accomplish next? Call me back, I really want to know. Bye!

When I started this course, I could only say a few words. In the first lessons, I learned to describe my neighborhood and my daily routine. Later, I talked about my goals and the milestones I want to reach. Along the way, I have overcome many of my mistakes. Now I am ready for the second half of my English journey.
